{{Short description="Composite particle made of quarks held together by the strong interaction}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Hadron
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quark]], [[Strong interaction]], [[Proton]], [[Neutron]], [[Elementary particle]]
| types = [[Baryon]]s (3 quarks), [[Meson]]s (quark–antiquark)
}}

In [[particle physics]], a '''hadron''' is a [[composite particle]] made of two or more [[quark]]s held together by the [[strong interaction]], analogous to how [[molecule]]s are held together by the [[electromagnetic force]]. The name "hadron" comes from the Greek ἁδρός (''hadros''), meaning "stout" or "thick."

== Classification ==

=== Baryons ===
{{Main|Baryon}}
Fermionic hadrons composed of three quarks:
* [[Proton]] (<math>uud</math>) — stable, charge +1
* [[Neutron]] (<math>udd</math>) — stable in nuclei, charge 0
* [[Lambda baryon]], [[Sigma baryon]], [[Xi baryon]], [[Omega baryon]] — heavier, unstable
* [[Antiproton]] (<math>\bar{u}\bar{u}\bar{d}</math>)

=== Mesons ===
{{Main|Meson}}
Bosonic hadrons composed of one quark and one antiquark (<math>q\bar{q}</math>):
* [[Pion]]s (<math>\pi^+, \pi^0, \pi^-</math>) — lightest mesons; mediate nuclear force
* [[Kaon]]s (<math>K^+, K^0, \bar{K}^0, K^-</math>) — contain strange quarks
* J/ψ meson (<math>c\bar{c}</math>) — charm–anticharm; discovery confirmed the [[charm quark]] (1974)

=== Exotic hadrons ===
* [[Tetraquark]]s — four quarks (<math>qq\bar{q}\bar{q}</math>)
* [[Pentaquark]]s — five quarks (<math>qqqq\bar{q}</math>)
* [[Glueball]]s — bound states of [[gluon]]s (predicted, not definitively observed)

== Properties ==
* Held together by the [[strong interaction]]
* Colour-neutral overall (white: no net [[colour charge]])
* Mass arises mainly from QCD binding energy, not quark rest masses (~99% of proton mass)
* Sizes: ~0.8 fm (proton radius)
* Most are unstable; only protons are stable (neutrons stable in nuclei)

== Hadronization ==
When quarks are produced in high-energy collisions, they cannot exist in isolation. They undergo [[hadronization]] — forming [[jet (particle physics)|jets]] of hadrons — due to [[colour confinement]].

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Baryon]]
* [[Gluon]]
* [[Meson]]
* [[Neutron]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Strong interaction]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Hadrons| ]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
[[Category:Quantum chromodynamics]]
